mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near zero (0.07%), the discount rate applied to equity cash flows remains structurally compressed, biasing the equity risk premium trade toward risk assets and producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following month as long as the funds rate stays near the zero lower bound.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.07 as of t-1mo, effectively at zero lower bound
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns are not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) during periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or rank IC of FEDFUNDS level with forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above the 9.0% threshold, it signals sustained labor market slack consistent with elevated recession risk; forward-looking equity markets price in weaker corporate earnings and consumer demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter until the unemployment rate begins a sustained decline.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of historical instances where UNRATE > 9.0%, or the sign concordance between UNRATE-triggered signal and realized direction is not statistically distinguishable from 50% (binomial test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where RSAFS (retail sales) continues to grow while HOUST (housing starts) simultaneously declines indicates an unbalanced consumption-led expansion masking underlying structural demand weakness in the interest-rate-sensitive housing sector; this joint pattern predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the following month as the housing drag eventually feeds through to broader growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=629.0 thousand units as of t-2mo, near post-crisis lows
failure_condition: When both conditions co-occur, 20-day forward SPX returns are not significantly negative (p>0.05) relative to unconditional baseline, or the joint interaction term fails to add predictive power over either single-series model (delta rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: In the immediate aftermath of the August 2011 debt-ceiling crisis and sovereign downgrade shock, any deceleration in INDPRO month-over-month growth signals fragile production momentum in a regime of impaired credit and confidence channels, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following trading week as markets remain hypersensitive to incremental growth data.
regime_hint: post-shock deleveraging aftermath following sovereign downgrade
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following sub-threshold INDPRO prints are not significantly negative versus unconditional 5-day return distribution (p>0.10), or the effect disappears outside the 2011 crisis window when tested walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation above 3.5% raises market expectations of future Fed tightening despite the current zero rate stance, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following trading week as rate-hike repricing occurs.
regime_hint: rising inflation pressure amid ultra-low policy rates
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns are not significantly negative when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3.5% (p>0.05), or the signal shows no differential effect from baseline across a 3-year rolling walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month trend in PAYEMS reflects continued private payroll expansion, functioning as a leading indicator of resilient corporate revenue and consumer income, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week as the labor trend confirms economic footing.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns show no significant positive relationship (rank IC < 0.03) with trailing 3-month PAYEMS momentum over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 quarterly growth decelerates while RSAFS simultaneously shows flat-to-negative monthly momentum, the joint signal indicates a broadening demand slowdown across both aggregate output and consumer spending channels, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as earnings estimates are revised downward across multiple sectors.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth < 1.5% AND RSAFS 3-month MoM average <= 0
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1=13270.1 as of t-4mo indicating sub-2% annualized growth in early 2011
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly negative under the joint condition versus either single-indicator baseline (delta rank IC < 0.02), or positive returns occur in more than 55% of historical joint-trigger instances.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is pinned at the zero lower bound concurrently with subdued CPIAUCSL inflation growth, the economy is in a liquidity-trap regime where conventional monetary easing loses transmission power, causing equity markets to underperform their typical ZIRP-driven rally over the following month due to persistent growth and deflation-risk concerns.
regime_hint: zero lower bound liquidity trap with disinflation risk
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns during joint ZLB-disinflation regime are not significantly lower than unconditional ZIRP-only baseline returns (p>0.05), invalidating the liquidity-trap dampening hypothesis.
